title: Komm Susser Tod Substitute
wikitext:
[]: Komm Susser Tod Substitute             Quote Post by LowerDefinition17 » Sun Jun 28, 2009 5:25 am If you had to pick a song for the 3rd impact / tang scene in EoE other than Komm Susser Tod, what song would you choose? I'd choose "We Will Become Silhouettes" by The Postal Service, because I think it fits the mood of the scenes and the lyrics tend to coincide with the action.

[]:             Quote Post by Marduk » Sun Jun 28, 2009 5:38 am Cold - End of the World

[]:             Quote Post by Cypher » Sun Jun 28, 2009 7:19 am Blue Monday by Orgy

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Sun Jun 28, 2009 8:22 am Radiohead - Street Spirit

[]:             Quote Post by Kinji » Sun Jun 28, 2009 11:41 am Some random up-beat Japanese song.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sun Jun 28, 2009 11:47 am The Entertainer

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jun 28, 2009 12:41 pm "It's the End of the World as We Know It (And I Feel Fine)" by R.E.M.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jun 28, 2009 1:12 pm I'd go with a slower, purely choral version of "What Becomes of the Broken Hearted" by Jimmy Ruffin .

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Jun 28, 2009 2:00 pm Cypher wrote: Blue Monday by Orgy

[]:             Quote Post by Orphan Of Darkness » Sun Jun 28, 2009 2:53 pm Silent Hill 4 soundtrack- Akira Yamaoka's "Room Of Angel"

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Jun 28, 2009 10:48 pm Crazy by Gnarls Barkley

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Mon Jun 29, 2009 12:58 am The End - The Doors That has to be the preeminent choice.

[]:             Quote Post by The Lincolnshire Poacher » Mon Jun 29, 2009 5:34 am Brain Damage/Eclipse - Pink Floyd

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jun 29, 2009 9:55 am Mr. Tines wrote: "It's the End of the World as We Know It (And I Feel Fine)" by R.E.M.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Mon Jun 29, 2009 10:48 am Pink Floyd's Dark Side of the Moon.... the whole Goddamned lot of it. Of course the tang scenes would have to be extended a little, but you could mix in the EOTV endings for no loss....

[]:             Quote Post by TheRatherDVD » Tue Jun 30, 2009 5:17 am Komm Susser Tod was a great song but I always thought Chicago by Sufjan Stevens fit the moment better.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Tue Jun 30, 2009 5:30 am Mr. Tines wrote: "It's the End of the World as We Know It (And I Feel Fine)" by R.E.M.

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Tue Jun 30, 2009 7:49 am Huh... "Welcome Home" by Coheed and Cambria might actually work out nicely, in a more hardcore sort of way... If I had to go for a similar feel to Komm Susser Tod, um... Maybe "Arigatou" by Flow? I don't know all of the lyrics, but it seems to have the same feel...

[]:             Quote Post by Captain_Morgan » Tue Jun 30, 2009 11:07 am The Lincolnshire Poacher wrote: Brain Damage/Eclipse - Pink Floyd

[]:             Quote Post by xfactor30seconds » Tue Jun 30, 2009 1:32 pm moonlight sonata just cause that goes with like every scene imaginable and maybe idk like something by placebo

I'd choose "We Will Become Silhouettes" by The Postal Service, because I think it fits the mood of the scenes and the lyrics tend to coincide with the action.
